title: Prioritize 12 project proposals competing for limited budget and resources
description: A mid-size financial services firm has 12 project proposals submitted for the next fiscal year but only budget for 7 and engineering capacity for 5 concurrent streams. Proposals range from regulatory mandates to innovation bets. The scoring model must produce a defensible ranking that satisfies the CFO (ROI focus), CTO (technical debt reduction), and Chief Risk Officer (compliance deadlines).

input.context: Financial services firm, $4.2M annual project budget, 38 engineers across 4 teams, 3 regulatory deadlines in Q2
input.constraints: Regulatory projects have hard Q2 deadlines. Core Banking Migration requires 12 senior engineers for 8 months.

expected_behavior: Scoring model includes at least 5 weighted criteria with stakeholder-agreed weights
Regulatory mandates (AML, PCI-DSS) rank at or near the top regardless of ROI
Sensitivity analysis shows which projects change rank under different weight scenarios
Clear funding cut-line recommendation with rationale for projects above and below
Resource contention identified for Core Banking Migration vs. other infrastructure projects

title: Portfolio rebalancing after strategic pivot — new CEO changes priorities
description: A technology company's new CEO shifts strategy from 'growth at all costs' to 'profitable growth with operational excellence.' The existing portfolio is 60% innovation/growth and only 15% efficiency/operations. The portfolio balancer must assess the imbalance, recommend which projects to accelerate, defer, or cancel, and produce a rebalanced portfolio aligned to the new strategy.

input.context: B2B SaaS company, $12M annual project portfolio, new CEO announced strategic pivot 3 weeks ago, board expects rebalanced portfolio within 30 days

expected_behavior: Current vs. target balance visualization across all 5 dimensions
Imbalance severity quantified with deviation percentages per dimension
Specific projects recommended for deferral or cancellation in innovation/growth categories
New efficiency/operations projects proposed or accelerated to fill the gap
Stakeholder communication plan for teams whose projects are affected
Transition timeline showing quarterly balance trajectory toward target

title: Dependency analysis revealing 3 projects blocked by 1 enabling project
description: During portfolio planning, the dependency mapper discovers that the 'Cloud Platform Migration' project is a hard blocker for 3 high-value downstream projects: 'AI Analytics Platform,' 'Customer 360 Data Lake,' and 'Real-Time Event Processing.' The Cloud Platform Migration is currently understaffed and 6 weeks behind schedule. The analysis must quantify cascade risk and recommend mitigation.

input.context: Enterprise retailer, 24-project portfolio, cloud-first strategy approved 9 months ago

expected_behavior: Dependency graph clearly shows the enabling project as a high-fan-out node blocking 3 projects
Cascade delay calculated: 6-week slip propagates to $4.3M combined value-at-risk
Impact scenarios modeled for 2, 4, and 8-week additional slippage
Mitigation recommendations: fast-track staffing, parallel workstreams, or interface decoupling
Critical path analysis identifies this chain as the longest in the portfolio
Escalation recommendation to governance with specific resource ask (2 additional cloud architects)

title: Portfolio optimization when 2 high-priority projects need the same architect
description: Two top-5 priority projects — 'Payment Gateway Replatforming' and 'Zero Trust Security Implementation' — both require the organization's only enterprise security architect for overlapping 4-month periods. Neither project can substitute a junior resource for this role. The optimizer must resolve the contention without dropping either project from the portfolio.

input.context: Fintech company, 15-project portfolio, 1 enterprise security architect (Maria Chen), both projects board-approved
input.constraint: No external hire possible within 3 months due to clearance requirements. Maria Chen is the sole enterprise security architect.

expected_behavior: Bottleneck clearly identified: Maria Chen as single-person dependency across 2 critical projects
At least 3 sequencing scenarios modeled with portfolio value curves
Scenario analysis includes: stagger projects, split Maria's time 50/50, or phase security architecture work
Recommended plan respects both hard deadlines (PCI in Sep, board mandate in Dec)
Fragile allocation flagged with risk mitigation: cross-training plan or contractor pipeline for future resilience
Re-optimization trigger defined if either project slips by more than 2 weeks
